data_source.description: Gold and silver normalized price ratio, 1968-2026. File: DATA/Commodities/gold_silver_normalized.csv

steps.step1_system: EXP-01: Gold/Silver Price Ratio: 2-part simplex, carriers=['Gold', 'Silver']

data_source.description: EMBER Global Electricity Review, yearly data for United States. Carriers aggregated: Fossil={Coal,Gas,Other Fossil}, Nuclear, Renewable={Hydro,Solar,Wind,Bioenergy,Other Renewables}. CC BY 4.0 license.

steps.step1_system: EXP-02: US Electricity Generation (Yearly): 3-part simplex, carriers=['Fossil', 'Nuclear', 'Renewable']

data_source.description: Semi-Empirical Mass Formula (SEMF/Weizsacker) decomposition of nuclear binding energy into [Volume, Surface+Coulomb, Symmetry+Pairing] for Z=1..92. AME2020 mass data from IAEA. File: DATA/Nuclear/raymond_semf_ratios.csv

steps.step1_system: EXP-03: Nuclear Binding Energy (SEMF): 3-part simplex, carriers=['Volume', 'Surf+Coulomb', 'Sym+Pairing']
EXP-04: Microphone Valley Response (Bessel J₁): 3-part simplex, carriers=['Low', 'Mid', 'High']

data_source.description: Synthetic basalt-to-rhyolite differentiation series. 150 samples along fractionation index 0→1. Based on standard TAS oxide fractionation trends (Le Maitre et al., 2002). Deterministic seed=42. Real data validation in EXP-05b (40,666 rocks).

DT fusion energy partition modeled across burn stages. 4-carrier system: [Kinetic products, Radiation losses, Neutron energy, Thermal losses]. Based on ITER/NIF energy budget literature. Deterministic seed=606.

steps.step1_system: EXP-06: Nuclear Fusion Energy Budget (DT): 4-part simplex, carriers=['Kinetic', 'Radiation', 'Neutron', 'Thermal']

data_source.description: Quark mass fractions + gluon binding energy for 8 hadrons (proton, neutron, pions, kaons, hyperons) plus QCD running coupling evolution (30 scale points). Based on PDG 2024 quark masses and lattice QCD binding estimates.

steps.step1_system: EXP-07: QCD Quark Compositions: 4-part simplex, carriers=['Up', 'Down', 'Strange', 'Binding']

data_source.description: Quark-gluon plasma freeze-out modeled as 3-part composition [Quarks, Gluons, Hadrons] across temperature range 400→100 MeV. Crossover at Tc=155 MeV (STAR/ALICE measurements). Sigmoid transition.

steps.step1_system: EXP-08: QGP Freeze-out Composition: 3-part simplex, carriers=['Quarks', 'Gluons', 'Hadrons']

data_source.description: Representative 3-part compositions from 6 domains (Energy, Nuclear, Chemistry, Finance, Acoustics, Geochemistry). 25 samples per domain, 150 total. Tests cross-domain pipeline stability.
Combined dataset: CKM quark mixing matrix (3 rows), PMNS neutrino mixing matrix (3 rows), SEMF binding energy decomposition for Z=2..92 (46 nuclei). Tests cross-domain universality.
Cosmological composition [Ω_m, Ω_r, Ω_Λ] at redshifts z=0→2 (30 points, Planck 2018 parameters) + stellar nucleosynthesis channels [pp-chain, CNO, triple-α] across stellar mass 0.5→25 M☉ (30 points).

steps.step1_system: EXP-11: Cosmology + Stellar Nucleosynthesis: 3-part simplex, carriers=['Channel_A', 'Channel_B', 'Channel_C']

data_source.description: GW150914 gravitational wave energy partition modeled as 3-phase composition [Inspiral, Merger, Ringdown]. 60 time steps. Based on LIGO/Virgo GW150914 waveform decomposition. GWOSC Open Science Center data.

steps.step1_system: EXP-12: GW150914 Phase Composition: 3-part simplex, carriers=['Inspiral', 'Merger', 'Ringdown']

data_source.description: QCD running coupling at 40 energy scales (1→1000 GeV) + stellar nucleosynthesis evolution (40 time steps). Tests for ILR parabolas on 2-simplex.

steps.step1_system: EXP-13: Parabola Hunter (QCD + Stellar): 3-part simplex, carriers=['Channel_1', 'Channel_2', 'Channel_3']

data_source.description: AME2020 Atomic Mass Evaluation — 3,554 nuclides with experimental masses. SEMF decomposition into [Volume, Surface+Coulomb, Symmetry]. File: DATA/Nuclear/ame2020_parsed.csv or DATA/ATOMIC/mass.mas20.txt

Cromer-Mann X-ray scattering factor coefficients for 10 crystal compositions (Olivine, Almandine, Perovskite, Calcite, Quartz — Fe, Mg, Al, Ca, Ti, C, Si, O atoms). 4-Gaussian decomposition at 20 scattering angles each. International Tables for Crystallography Vol C.

Spring-mass system with gravity and viscous damping. m=5kg, k=200N/m, c=2N·s/m, g=9.81m/s², x₀=0.3m. RK4 integration, 1000 timesteps over 10s. Force decomposition: |F_spring|, |F_damping|, F_gravity, |F_inertia|.

steps.step1_system: EXP-16: Spring-Mass Force Composition: 4-part simplex, carriers=['Spring', 'Damping', 'Gravity', 'Inertia']

data_source.description: Coupled harmonic oscillators with D=4 carriers. Natural frequencies ω=[1,2,3,4]. 500 timesteps over 20s. Tests Compositional Uncertainty Principle: floor = 6.37 × N^0.18.

steps.step1_system: EXP-17: Compositional Uncertainty Principle (D=4): 4-part simplex, carriers=['Carrier_1', 'Carrier_2', 'Carrier_3', 'Carrier_4']

data_source.description: AISI 1020 mild steel stress-strain decomposition. E=200GPa, σ_y=350MPa, K=600MPa, n=0.2. Force 3-part: [Elastic, Plastic, Thermal dissipation]. 100 strain points, 0→50%. Ramberg-Osgood model.

steps.step1_system: EXP-18: Stress-Strain Force Composition (AISI 1020): 3-part simplex, carriers=['Elastic', 'Plastic', 'Thermal']

data_source.description: Two bridge experiments: Bridge 5 (Energy↔Force): Charged droplet in crossed E+B fields, 50 time steps. 3-part: [E-field energy, B-field energy, Kinetic]. Bridge 6 (Matter↔Gravity): Gravitational sedimentation, 30 time steps. 3-part: [Gravity pull, Buoyancy, Viscous drag]. Completes K₄ complete graph of {Energy, Matter, Force, Gravity}.

steps.step1_system: EXP-18b: K₄ Bridge Completion: 3-part simplex, carriers=['Field_A', 'Field_B', 'Kinetic']

EXP-19.name: Fourier Conjugate Pair Study
EXP-19.self_conjugate_preservation: PERFECT for Gauss, shah, sech (all ratios = 1.000)
EXP-19.reverse_test: Pair 02 (tri↔sinc²) and Pair 10 (sinc²↔tri) produce exact swapped metrics

EXP-19b.title: Pipeline Optimization via Conjugate Pair Validation

euler_family_discovery.catalyst: Peter Higgins: 'I do not trust loss of test and data'
euler_family_discovery.description: Retaining all 28 transcendental constants (rejecting OPT-C pruning) led to discovery that Euler-family constants 2π, e^π, π^e are closest super squeeze matches in real-world experimental data
euler_family_discovery.mathematical_connection: Pipeline log→complex→polar maps to Euler identity e^(iπ)=-1: 2π=angular period (helix step), e^π=CLR↔circular junction (Gelfond's constant), π^e=PLL balance point (Gelfond's conjugate)
euler_family_discovery.significance: POINT OF INTEREST — cross-domain confirmation across 44 orders of magnitude (commodities, nuclear SEMF, nuclear AME, QCD, gravity)

EXP-20.title: Dielectric Function Decomposition
EXP-20.result: NATURAL. 4 decompositions of hBN permittivity. ln(φ) at δ=0.000012, 1/(2π) at δ=0.000029.

metadata.exp04_note: EXP-04 re-run 2026-04-22 with proper Bessel J₁ via Miller backward recurrence. Previous run used sinc(ka)=sin(ka)/ka approximation, flagged as Fourier pair concern (sinc↔rect). J₁ does not form a conjugate pair with the EITT rectangular window.
